Tests if `undefined` is strictly equal to `void 0`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion statement that checks if the value `undefined` is strictly equal to the result of the `void 0` expression. The `void` operator evaluates the given expression and returns `undefined`, making this assertion always true. This code serves as a basic test to confirm the behavior of the `void` operator in JavaScript. It provides narrow functionality, focusing on verifying a specific language feature.
